scaled_oracle_cases.description: Transformed seed case with mixed data (None, negative, strings) to test data cleaning, sorted output, and filter logic.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function handles None, negative, and string-encoded numbers, and returns sorted values and correct probabilities.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Input containing None should be ignored, negative values discarded, strings converted to int; output values must be sorted in ascending order.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed solution will raise TypeError on None or fail to convert strings, or produce unsorted output.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
datalist = [8]*200 + [None]*10 + [-5]*5 + ['10']*120 + [12]*150 + [15]*90 + [20]*50
values, probs = compute_probs(datalist)
print("Selected fragment lengths:", values)
print("Their probabilities:", probs)
result = {'values': values, 'probs': probs}
covered_requirements: Introduce data cleaning requirements: handle missing values (None), invalid negative lengths, and string-encoded numbers. Also require output sorting.
Add requirement for sorted output and proper handling of edge cases like empty input or no passing lengths.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new data cleaning requirement introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new edge-case handling and ordering constraint introduced by this operator.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Verify that output values are sorted even if input order is different.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Ensure that the values list is sorted in ascending order regardless of input order.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Output values must be sorted in ascending order.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed solution may output values in arbitrary order based on set iteration.
